question_text: A rubber band is stretched beyond its elastic limit. What will happen when you release it?

options.label: A
options.text: It will return to its original shape perfectly

options.label: B
options.text: It will not return to its original shape and will remain stretched

options.label: C
options.text: It will become shorter than its original length

correct_answer: It will not return to its original shape and will remain stretched
explanation: When a material is stretched beyond its elastic limit, it undergoes permanent (plastic) deformation. The material loses its elasticity and cannot return to its original shape when the force is removed. This is why rubber bands that are over-stretched become loose and floppy.
